thresholds._note: Capability-entity-conflict rows: an entity strongly associated with domain A is named while the CAPABILITY asked belongs to domain B, requested by a persona NOT entitled to domain B. Two things must hold: (1) routing keys on the CAPABILITY (expected_goal is the domain-B capability, scored as a flat pick — capability-first routing, not entity-first), and (2) that capability is DENIED for this persona (expected_denied_capability must land in a denied/partial group). There are no clear/out-of-scope rows here, so only the denial expectations gate. Test data — domain literals are allowed (CLAUDE.md §5); the gateway Java carries none.

queries.query: Show pending and failed settlements for Continental Freight.
queries.note: uw_sam holds the insurance segment only. 'settlements' is an asset-servicing capability; routing must pick settlement_status (capability-first) and structural authz must DENY it for an insurance-only persona — never silently substitute an insurance capability because the persona is an underwriter.

queries.query: What is the insurance renewal risk for the Whitman Family Office?
queries.note: Whitman is a wealth/servicing relationship rm_jane covers, but 'insurance renewal risk' is an insurance capability rm_jane's segments (wealth+servicing) do not authorize. Route to renewal_risk (capability-first) and DENY — do not fall back to a wealth capability just because the entity is a wealth client.

queries.query: Pull the wealth portfolio holdings for the Whitman Family Office.
queries.note: A wealth capability requested by an insurance-only persona about a wealth entity. Route to holdings (capability-first), DENY structurally.

queries.query: Give me the insurance renewal risk for REL-00188.
queries.note: ops_analyst_singh holds the servicing segment. An insurance capability keyed off a servicing relationship id must route to renewal_risk and DENY — the servicing entitlement does not spill into insurance.
